mechanism_details.description_snippet: ...anisms of Action The antimicrobial activity of AMPs primarily arises from their interactions with microbial cell membranes, a process that typically involves two stages: electrostatic attraction and membrane disruption or translocation. 2.1.1 Membrane-Targeting Mechanisms The cationic nature of AMPs enables preferential binding to negatively charged bacterial membranes, which are enriched in phosphatidylglycerol...

...hicity Hydrophobicity governs the ability of AMPs to insert into lipid bilayers, while amphipathicity—the spatial segregation of hydrophobic and hydrophilic residues—enables membrane interaction and pore formation. ...been proposed, wherein the amphiphilic peptides dissolve the lipid bilayer, yielding micellar or bicellar membrane patches. Finally, peptides can also increase permeability of the membrane by direct pore formation. Such pores can be lined by the peptide as in the “barrel-stave” model, or the peptides can stabilize a pore that is intrinsically formed by lipids according to the“wormhole”model.
